title: $1,000/Year in S&P 500 Starting at Age 18
description: What investing $1,000 every January in the S&P 500 from age 18 (year 2004) would be worth today at age 39 (year 2025). Total invested: $21,000. This scenario models the simplest possible consistent investing habit — one investment per year, same amount, same index fund.
vs_cash_note: Keeping $1,000/year in cash would give $21,000 today. Investing gives ~$67,000 — an additional $46,000 from compounding.
educational_note: The first $1,000 invested in 2004 had 21 years to compound. The last $1,000 invested in 2025 has had almost no time. This illustrates why starting early matters more than investing large amounts later — each year of compounding is irreplaceable.
future_projection_note: Projections beyond today are NOT included. This dataset only covers historical, verified data.
